mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and signals easier liquidity conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is trending down for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still rising, policy restraint and sticky inflation compress real liquidity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change remains positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the sign weakens when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% but CPIAUCSL is decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and higher recession probability, so equity prices should fall over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if a 3-month increase in UNRATE is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in PAYEMS above recent trend confirms labor-market resilience and supports consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-surprise-like upturns in PAYEMS are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal reverses when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad final demand and housing activity, implying stronger near-term growth expectations and higher S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upside moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to outperform single-series effects, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive after both series rise versus their 3-month averages.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and profit-sensitive cyclicals, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if sub-trend INDPRO readings are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the relationship vanishes outside manufacturing slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDP growth surprise relative to the prior quarter signals broad demand weakness and lower earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth does not coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent when growth decelerates but stays positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated and FEDFUNDS is still rising, real policy becomes tighter, discount rates increase, and equity returns should be negative over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises month over month while CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-plus-tightening condition does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the effect weakens when inflation is elevated but FEDFUNDS is flat.
